title: Asia — Physical Features & Climate
highYieldFacts: Asia is the largest continent, occupying about one-third of Earth's land area.
Asia extends from the Arctic Ocean in the north to the Indian Ocean in the south.
The Pacific Ocean forms Asia's eastern boundary; the Mediterranean and Ural chain form the west.
Europe–Asia boundary: Ural Mountains, Ural River, Caspian Sea, Caucasus, Black Sea.
Bering Strait separates Asia from North America.
Extremes: Cape Chelyuskin (north), Cape Piai (south), Cape Baba (west), Cape Dezhnev (east).
Asia's physical divisions: Northern Lowlands, Central Highlands, Southern Plateaus/Plains.
Pamir knot is a junction where major Asian ranges meet.
Himalayas are young fold mountains and act as a climatic barrier.
Tibetan Plateau is the highest and largest plateau in the world.
Indo-Gangetic Plain is one of the most fertile alluvial plains.
West Siberian Plain is one of the largest lowlands on Earth.
Major Arctic rivers: Ob, Yenisei, Lena.
Major Pacific rivers: Yangtze, Huang He, Mekong, Amur.
Major Indian Ocean rivers: Indus, Ganga, Brahmaputra.
Tigris and Euphrates drain into the Persian Gulf.
Caspian Sea is the world's largest inland lake.
Lake Baikal is the world's deepest freshwater lake.
Aral Sea has shrunk due to diversion of its feeder rivers.
Asia has climate zones from tundra to equatorial rainforest.
Continentality causes extreme temperature ranges in Central Asia.
Altitude lowers temperature; high mountains block moist winds.
Monsoon is a seasonal reversal of winds, not just rainfall.
South-west monsoon brings summer rain to South Asia.
North-east monsoon is dry; Tamil Nadu gets winter rain from it.
Western disturbances bring winter rain to north-west India and Pakistan.
Typhoons are tropical cyclones over the western Pacific.
Tundra vegetation includes mosses and lichens; taiga has conifers.
Steppe grasslands dominate semi-arid interiors of Central Asia.
Mangroves thrive in delta regions like the Sundarbans.

notes.content:
**Executive summary:** Asia is the largest continent, stretching from the Arctic Ocean in the north to the Indian Ocean in the south and from the Mediterranean in the west to the Pacific in the east.

**Must know**
• Largest continent: about one-third of Earth's land area
• Northern boundary: Arctic Ocean; southern boundary: Indian Ocean
• Western boundary: Ural Mountains, Ural River, Caspian Sea, Caucasus, Black Sea
• Eastern boundary: Pacific Ocean; north-east separated from North America by Bering Strait
• Extremes: Cape Chelyuskin (north), Cape Piai (south), Cape Baba (west), Cape Dezhnev (east)

Asia occupies the eastern and northern parts of the land hemisphere. It extends from the Arctic Ocean in the north to the Indian Ocean in the south, and from the Mediterranean Sea and Ural Mountains in the west to the Pacific Ocean in the east. Asia is separated from Europe by the Ural Mountains, Ural River, the Caspian Sea, the Caucasus Mountains and the Black Sea. The Bering Strait separates Asia from North America. Its extreme points include Cape Chelyuskin in the north, Cape Piai in the south, Cape Baba in the west and Cape Dezhnev in the east. This vast latitudinal span explains Asia's enormous climatic variety.
notes.explanation:
Remember boundary features in a west-to-east chain. ICSE often asks for the Europe–Asia boundary and the oceans that surround Asia.

**🔗 Links:** geo-rev-ch6-02 — next topic
notes.teacherTip: Ask students to trace Asia on a world map and mark the four extreme capes for spatial memory.
notes.examTip: Always mention the Ural–Caspian–Caucasus–Black Sea line when asked about Europe–Asia boundary.

notes.content:
**Executive summary:** Asia has three broad physical divisions: Northern Lowlands, Central Highlands and Southern Plateaus/Plains.

**Must know**
• Northern Lowlands: West Siberian Plain and Turan Lowland
• Central Highlands: mountains and plateaus—Pamir, Tien Shan, Kunlun, Himalayas
• Southern Plateaus: Arabian and Deccan plateaus; Indo-Gangetic and Chinese plains
• Central Highlands act as a climate barrier
• Plains are densely populated and agriculturally rich

Asia can be broadly divided into three physical divisions. The Northern Lowlands include the West Siberian Plain and Turan Lowland, drained by rivers like Ob, Yenisei and Lena. The Central Highlands comprise the world's highest mountains and plateaus—Pamir (the 'Roof of the World'), Tien Shan, Kunlun, Karakoram and the Himalayas. These highlands influence river systems and block moist winds. The Southern Plateaus and Plains include the Arabian and Deccan plateaus, and vast alluvial plains such as the Indo-Gangetic and North China plains. These plains support dense populations and intensive agriculture.
notes.explanation:
Use the division scheme as: North = lowlands, Centre = mountains/plateaus, South = plateaus/plains. Connect landforms to climate and population.

**🔗 Links:** geo-rev-ch6-01 — previous topic; geo-rev-ch6-03 — next topic
notes.teacherTip: Use a physical map to show how highlands create rain shadow and river sources.
notes.examTip: Mention at least one example in each division for full marks.

notes.content:
**Executive summary:** Asia's mountain systems include the Himalayas, Karakoram, Hindu Kush, Tien Shan, Kunlun, Altai, Zagros and Arakan Yoma ranges.

**Must know**
• Himalayas: world's highest mountain system, young fold mountains
• Karakoram and Hindu Kush meet near the Pamir knot
• Tien Shan and Kunlun form inner Asian ranges
• Altai separates Siberia from Central Asia
• Zagros in West Asia; Arakan Yoma in Myanmar

Asia is dominated by massive mountain systems. The Himalayas, the world's highest young fold mountains, extend over 2,400 km and influence climate by blocking cold winds from Central Asia. The Karakoram and Hindu Kush ranges meet the Himalayas near the Pamir knot. Inner Asian ranges include the Tien Shan and Kunlun. In the north-west, the Altai range separates Siberia from Central Asia. West Asia is marked by the Zagros mountains, while the Arakan Yoma runs along Myanmar's western border. These mountains are sources of major rivers and create natural barriers affecting climate and human settlement.
notes.explanation:
Mountains are not just relief features; they are climatic barriers and river sources. The Pamir knot is a key junction where ranges meet.

**🔗 Links:** geo-rev-ch6-02 — previous topic; geo-rev-ch6-04 — next topic
notes.teacherTip: Use a 'spokes from Pamir' diagram to show how ranges radiate from Central Asia.
notes.examTip: State why Himalayas are vital: they block cold winds and help create monsoon rainfall.

notes.content:
**Executive summary:** Asia has major plateaus like Tibet, Iranian and Deccan and plains like the Indo-Gangetic, North China and West Siberian plains.

**Must know**
• Tibetan Plateau: highest and largest plateau, source of major rivers
• Iranian and Anatolian plateaus dominate West Asia
• Deccan Plateau forms peninsular India
• Indo-Gangetic Plain: one of the world's most fertile plains
• North China and Manchurian plains support dense populations

Asia's plateaus are extensive. The Tibetan Plateau, the highest and largest, feeds rivers such as the Yangtze, Yellow and Mekong. The Iranian and Anatolian plateaus shape West Asia's arid climate. The Deccan Plateau forms peninsular India, flanked by the Western and Eastern Ghats. Asia's plains include the Indo-Gangetic Plain, formed by the Indus-Ganga-Brahmaputra system; the North China Plain formed by the Huang He and Yangtze; and the West Siberian Plain, one of the world's largest lowlands. These plains are fertile due to alluvium and support dense populations and agriculture.
notes.explanation:
Plateaus often create rain shadows, while plains are fertile and densely populated—link landforms to settlement patterns.

**🔗 Links:** geo-rev-ch6-03 — previous topic; geo-rev-ch6-05 — next topic
notes.teacherTip: Compare Tibetan Plateau (source) with Indo-Gangetic Plain (deposition) to show river work.
notes.examTip: Use the phrase 'alluvial plains' when describing Indo-Gangetic and North China plains.

notes.content:
**Executive summary:** Asia's rivers drain into the Arctic, Pacific and Indian oceans, while lakes like the Caspian, Baikal and Aral are key inland water bodies.

**Must know**
• Major Arctic-draining rivers: Ob, Yenisei, Lena
• Pacific-draining rivers: Yangtze, Huang He, Mekong, Amur
• Indian Ocean system: Indus, Ganga, Brahmaputra, Tigris-Euphrates
• Caspian Sea: world's largest inland lake
• Lake Baikal: world's deepest freshwater lake

Asia's rivers are among the longest in the world. The Ob, Yenisei and Lena flow north into the Arctic Ocean. The Yangtze, Huang He, Mekong and Amur flow east into the Pacific. The Indus, Ganga and Brahmaputra flow south into the Indian Ocean, while the Tigris and Euphrates drain into the Persian Gulf. Asia also has important lakes: the Caspian Sea is the world's largest inland lake; Lake Baikal is the deepest freshwater lake; and the Aral Sea has shrunk drastically due to river diversion. These rivers and lakes sustain agriculture, transport and hydropower.
notes.explanation:
Classify rivers by drainage basin—Arctic, Pacific, Indian. Lakes are often asked for superlatives (largest, deepest).

**🔗 Links:** geo-rev-ch6-04 — previous topic; geo-rev-ch6-06 — next topic
notes.teacherTip: Ask students to match rivers with the ocean they drain into to strengthen map skills.
notes.examTip: Remember: Caspian = largest, Baikal = deepest; Aral Sea shrinkage is a noted human-impact case.

notes.content:
**Executive summary:** Asia's climate varies due to latitude, altitude, distance from sea, winds and ocean currents, creating zones from tundra to equatorial rainforest.

**Must know**
• Latitude controls temperature from Arctic to equatorial belt
• Altitude and relief: high mountains are colder and act as barriers
• Continentality: interiors have extreme temperature ranges
• Pressure belts and wind systems shape rainfall patterns
• Zones include tundra, taiga, temperate, steppe, desert, Mediterranean, tropical monsoon and equatorial

Asia's vast size and varied relief create multiple climate zones. Latitude determines basic temperature, from polar north to tropical south. Altitude reduces temperature in the Himalayas and Tibetan Plateau. Distance from sea increases continentality, causing hot summers and cold winters in the interior. Pressure belts, prevailing winds and ocean currents influence rainfall patterns. As a result, Asia has tundra and taiga in Siberia, temperate climates in East Asia, steppe and desert climates in Central and West Asia, Mediterranean climate around the Levant and Turkey, tropical monsoon climate in South and Southeast Asia, and equatorial climate in parts of Indonesia and Malaysia.
notes.explanation:
Always connect factor → effect. For example, continentality leads to extreme seasonal temperature ranges.

**🔗 Links:** geo-rev-ch6-05 — previous topic; geo-rev-ch6-07 — next topic
notes.teacherTip: Use a climate zone map and link each zone to vegetation types for integrated learning.
notes.examTip: List 3–4 climate factors before describing climate zones in any answer.

notes.content:
**Executive summary:** The monsoon is a seasonal reversal of winds; it brings heavy summer rain to South Asia and shapes regional climates across Asia.

**Must know**
• South-west monsoon: summer winds from sea to land bring rain
• North-east monsoon: winter winds from land to sea are dry
• Typhoons affect East and Southeast Asia
• Western disturbances bring winter rain to north-west India and Pakistan
• Orographic rainfall on windward slopes creates rain shadow on leeward side

Monsoon winds are seasonal reversals caused by differential heating of land and sea. In summer, the south-west monsoon blows from the Indian Ocean to the land, bringing heavy rainfall to South and Southeast Asia. In winter, the north-east monsoon blows from land to sea, bringing dry conditions, though Tamil Nadu receives winter rain from the Bay of Bengal branch. East Asia experiences typhoons—tropical cyclones over the Pacific. Western disturbances from the Mediterranean bring winter rain and snowfall to north-west India and Pakistan. Mountains like the Himalayas enhance orographic rainfall on windward slopes and create rain shadow areas.
notes.explanation:
Monsoon is a wind system, not just rain. ICSE expects seasonal wind direction and its consequences.

**🔗 Links:** geo-rev-ch6-06 — previous topic; geo-rev-ch6-08 — next topic
notes.teacherTip: Draw a simple summer vs winter wind diagram to show reversal clearly.
notes.examTip: Mention western disturbances for winter rainfall in Punjab and Kashmir—often asked.

notes.content:
**Executive summary:** Asia's vegetation ranges from tundra mosses in the Arctic to equatorial rainforests and mangroves in the tropics.

**Must know**
• Tundra: mosses, lichens, dwarf shrubs in the far north
• Taiga: coniferous forests (pine, spruce) in Siberia
• Temperate deciduous forests in China, Japan and Korea
• Steppe grasslands and desert vegetation in Central and West Asia
• Tropical monsoon forests, rainforests and mangroves in South and Southeast Asia

Vegetation in Asia reflects climate. The Arctic north has tundra with mosses and lichens. South of it lies the taiga or coniferous forest belt across Siberia. Temperate deciduous forests occur in China, Korea and Japan. The interiors of Central Asia have steppe grasslands and deserts with xerophytic shrubs. In South and Southeast Asia, tropical monsoon forests grow where summer rainfall is high, and equatorial rainforests flourish in Malaysia and Indonesia. Mangroves occur in delta regions such as the Sundarbans, adapted to saline conditions.
notes.explanation:
Link each vegetation type to its climate: cold → tundra/taiga, dry → steppe/desert, wet tropics → rainforests.

**🔗 Links:** geo-rev-ch6-07 — previous topic
notes.teacherTip: Use a climate–vegetation pairing chart for quick revision.
notes.examTip: Write the term 'xerophytic' for desert plants and 'mangroves' for delta coasts.

questions.question: Asia is located mainly in the:
questions.answer: Asia lies largely in the Northern and Eastern Hemispheres, though some islands extend into the Southern Hemisphere.

questions.question: Which ocean lies to the north of Asia?
questions.answer: The Arctic Ocean forms the northern boundary of Asia.
questions.examTip: Boundary oceans are basic map questions.

questions.question: The Europe–Asia boundary does NOT include the:
questions.answer: The Rockies are in North America; the Europe–Asia boundary uses Urals, Caspian and Caucasus.

questions.question: Asia is separated from North America by the:
questions.answer: The narrow Bering Strait separates Asia from North America (Russia–Alaska).

questions.question: Which is the southernmost point of mainland Asia?
questions.answer: Cape Piai (Tanjung Piai) in Malaysia is Asia's southernmost point.

questions.question: The westernmost point of Asia is:
questions.answer: Cape Baba in Turkey marks Asia's westernmost point.

questions.question: The easternmost point of Asia is:
questions.answer: Cape Dezhnev in Russia is Asia's easternmost point.

questions.question: The Pamir knot is important because it:
options: Is a junction of major mountain ranges
questions.answer: The Pamir knot is where major Asian mountain ranges such as the Himalayas, Karakoram and Tien Shan meet.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT part of Asia's Central Highlands?
questions.answer: The Appalachians are in North America; Tien Shan, Kunlun and Himalayas are in Asia.

questions.question: The Northern Lowlands of Asia include the:
questions.answer: The West Siberian Plain is a major part of Asia's Northern Lowlands.

questions.question: The highest and largest plateau of the world is:
questions.answer: The Tibetan Plateau is the highest and largest plateau on Earth.

questions.question: The Indo-Gangetic Plain is formed by the rivers:
questions.answer: The Indo-Gangetic Plain is formed by alluvium from Indus, Ganga and Brahmaputra rivers.

questions.question: Which mountain range forms the southern edge of the Tibetan Plateau?
questions.answer: The Himalayas form the southern edge of the Tibetan Plateau.

questions.question: The Karakoram range is best known for containing:
questions.answer: K2, the second-highest peak, lies in the Karakoram range.

questions.question: The Arakan Yoma range is located in:
questions.answer: The Arakan Yoma runs along western Myanmar (Burma).

questions.question: The Zagros Mountains are located in:
questions.answer: The Zagros mountains lie mainly in Iran and extend into Iraq.

questions.question: Which river drains into the Arctic Ocean?
questions.answer: The Lena River flows north into the Arctic Ocean.
questions.examTip: Ob, Yenisei, Lena are Arctic rivers.

questions.question: Which river flows into the Pacific Ocean?
questions.answer: The Yangtze River flows east into the Pacific Ocean.
questions.examTip: Pacific basin = Yangtze, Huang He, Mekong, Amur.

questions.question: Which river drains into the Persian Gulf?
questions.answer: The Euphrates (with Tigris) drains into the Persian Gulf.

questions.question: The largest inland lake in the world is:
questions.answer: The Caspian Sea is the world's largest inland lake by area.

questions.question: The deepest freshwater lake in the world is:
questions.answer: Lake Baikal in Siberia is the world's deepest freshwater lake.

questions.question: Which lake has shrunk significantly due to diversion of rivers?
questions.answer: The Aral Sea shrank drastically after its feeder rivers were diverted for irrigation.

questions.question: Which is a correct pair of river and drainage basin?
questions.answer: The Ob River drains into the Arctic Ocean.

questions.question: Which of the following is a young fold mountain system in Asia?
questions.answer: The Himalayas are young fold mountains formed by the Indo-Eurasian collision.

questions.question: The 'Roof of the World' refers to:
questions.answer: The Pamir Plateau is called the 'Roof of the World' due to its great height.

questions.question: The West Siberian Plain is significant because it is:
questions.answer: The West Siberian Plain is one of the largest lowlands on Earth.

questions.question: Which physical division contains most of Asia's high mountains?
questions.answer: The Central Highlands contain the major mountain systems and plateaus.

questions.question: Which factor best explains extreme temperature ranges in Central Asia?
questions.answer: Distance from the sea causes continentality, producing hot summers and cold winters.

questions.question: Altitude affects climate by:
questions.answer: Temperature decreases with height, so high plateaus and mountains are colder.

questions.question: Which climate zone is typical of northern Siberia?
questions.answer: Northern Siberia has tundra climate with permafrost and short summers.

questions.question: The taiga region is characterized by:
questions.answer: Taiga is a belt of coniferous forests (pine, spruce, fir) south of tundra.

questions.question: Which climate is associated with the Mediterranean coast of West Asia?
questions.answer: The Levant and Turkey have a Mediterranean climate with winter rain and dry summer.

questions.question: Which wind brings summer rainfall to South Asia?
questions.answer: The south-west monsoon blows from sea to land in summer, bringing heavy rains.

questions.question: The north-east monsoon is generally:
options: Dry, blowing from land to sea
questions.answer: The north-east monsoon blows from land to sea and is mostly dry, except along Tamil Nadu coast.

questions.question: Western disturbances mainly bring:
options: Winter rain and snowfall to NW India
questions.answer: Western disturbances bring winter rain and snow to north-west India and Pakistan.

questions.question: Typhoons are tropical cyclones over the:
questions.answer: Typhoons occur over the western Pacific, affecting East and Southeast Asia.

questions.question: Orographic rainfall occurs when:
options: Moist air is forced to rise over mountains
Cold air replaces warm air at sea
Air moves from land to sea in winter
questions.answer: Moist air rises over mountains, cools and condenses, producing orographic rainfall.

questions.question: The rain shadow effect is best seen:
questions.answer: Leeward slopes receive little rainfall because air descends and warms, creating rain shadow.

questions.question: Which plain is formed by the Huang He and Yangtze rivers?
questions.answer: The North China Plain is formed by the Huang He (Yellow) and Yangtze rivers.

questions.question: The Mesopotamian Plain is located between the rivers:
questions.answer: Mesopotamia lies between the Tigris and Euphrates rivers.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a plateau of Asia?
questions.answer: The Brazilian Plateau is in South America, not Asia.

questions.question: The Deccan Plateau is located in:
questions.answer: The Deccan Plateau is in peninsular India, part of South Asia.

questions.question: Which river is known as the 'Sorrow of China'?
questions.answer: The Huang He (Yellow River) is called the 'Sorrow of China' due to its floods.

questions.question: Which is the correct order from north to south in Asia?
questions.answer: From Arctic southwards: tundra, taiga, steppe/desert, then tropical zones.

questions.question: Which climatic factor best explains heavy rainfall on the windward side of the Western Ghats?
questions.answer: Moist monsoon winds rise over the Western Ghats, causing orographic rainfall.

questions.question: Which vegetation type is common in Central Asia's semi-arid regions?
questions.answer: Semi-arid interiors support steppe grasslands.

questions.question: Mangroves are best found in:
questions.answer: Mangroves grow in saline, tidal delta regions such as the Sundarbans.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Pacific-draining river of Asia?
questions.answer: The Mekong drains into the South China Sea (Pacific).

questions.question: Which pair correctly matches mountain range with region?
questions.answer: The Altai range separates Siberia from Central Asia.

questions.question: The Central Highlands influence climate by:
options: Allowing moist winds to pass freely
Blocking winds and creating rain shadow zones
questions.answer: High mountains block winds, create rain shadows and affect rainfall distribution.

questions.question: Which sea is part of the Europe–Asia boundary?
questions.answer: The Caspian Sea is part of the Europe–Asia boundary chain.

questions.question: Which river is the longest in Asia?
questions.answer: The Yangtze (Chang Jiang) is Asia's longest river.

questions.question: Which lake is below sea level and highly saline?
questions.answer: The Dead Sea is below sea level and extremely saline.

questions.question: Which climate is typical of West Asia's deserts?
questions.answer: West Asia is dominated by arid desert climate due to continentality and subtropical highs.

questions.question: Which statement about monsoon is correct?
options: It is a permanent wind system with no seasonal change
It is a seasonal reversal of winds
It is caused by ocean currents alone
questions.answer: Monsoon winds reverse direction seasonally due to differential heating of land and sea.

questions.question: Which is a correct river–plain match?
questions.answer: The Indo-Gangetic Plain is formed by the Ganga system (with Indus and Brahmaputra).

questions.question: The Himalayas help South Asia by:
options: Allowing cold winds from Central Asia
Blocking cold winds and enhancing monsoon rainfall
questions.answer: The Himalayas block cold continental winds and force moist monsoon winds to rise, causing rainfall.

questions.question: Which river is known for its delta forming the Sundarbans?
questions.answer: The Ganga-Brahmaputra delta forms the Sundarbans mangrove region.

questions.question: The climate of Japan is mainly:
questions.answer: Japan has a temperate climate moderated by the sea and affected by monsoon winds.

questions.question: Which vegetation type is typical of Siberia?
questions.answer: Siberia is dominated by taiga—coniferous forests.

questions.question: The West Siberian rivers flow mainly:
questions.answer: Ob, Yenisei and Lena flow north to the Arctic Ocean.

questions.question: Which region experiences Mediterranean climate in Asia?
questions.answer: The Mediterranean climate occurs along the Mediterranean coast of Turkey, Syria and Lebanon.

questions.question: Which statement about the Tibetan Plateau is correct?
options: It is the highest plateau and source of major rivers
questions.answer: The Tibetan Plateau is the highest plateau and source of major Asian rivers.

questions.question: Which wind system affects East Asia's summer rainfall besides monsoon?
questions.answer: East Asia receives summer rain from monsoon winds and typhoon systems from the Pacific.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Central Asian desert?
questions.answer: The Gobi Desert lies in Mongolia and northern China.

questions.question: The cold current affecting East Asia is:
questions.answer: The Oyashio is a cold current off Japan, while Kuroshio is warm.

questions.question: Which of the following is a correct sequence from west to east within Asia?
options: Mediterranean coast → Ural Mountains → Pacific Ocean
Pacific Ocean → Ural Mountains → Mediterranean coast
Ural Mountains → Pacific Ocean → Mediterranean coast
Arctic Ocean → Ural Mountains → Indian Ocean
questions.answer: From west to east: Mediterranean region and Ural boundary toward the Pacific coast.

questions.question: Which area has equatorial climate in Asia?
questions.answer: Equatorial climate occurs near the equator in Indonesia and Malaysia.

questions.question: The Indo-Gangetic Plain is MOST densely populated because:
options: It has fertile alluvium and abundant water
questions.answer: Fertile alluvium, gentle relief and ample water support dense agriculture and settlements.

questions.question: Which of the following is a correct vegetation–climate pair?
questions.answer: Taiga corresponds to subarctic climate with long cold winters.

questions.question: Asia is separated from Europe by the Ural Mountains, Ural River, Caspian Sea, Caucasus and Black Sea.
questions.answer: These physical features form the conventional Europe–Asia boundary.

questions.question: The Tibetan Plateau is the lowest plateau in the world.
questions.answer: It is the highest and largest plateau on Earth.

questions.question: The Ob, Yenisei and Lena drain into the Arctic Ocean.
questions.answer: These Siberian rivers flow northward to the Arctic.

questions.question: The north-east monsoon brings heavy rain to most of India in summer.
questions.answer: The south-west monsoon brings summer rain; the north-east monsoon is mostly dry.

questions.question: The Caspian Sea is the world's largest inland lake.
questions.answer: It is the largest by area.

questions.question: Steppe grasslands are found in the equatorial regions of Asia.
questions.answer: Steppes occur in semi-arid interiors of Central Asia.

questions.question: Typhoons are tropical cyclones of the western Pacific.
questions.answer: They affect East and Southeast Asia.

questions.question: The Himalayas block cold winds from Central Asia.
questions.answer: They act as a climatic barrier.

questions.question: Lake Baikal is the deepest freshwater lake in the world.
questions.answer: It holds the record for depth among freshwater lakes.

questions.question: Continentality reduces the seasonal temperature range of Central Asia.
questions.answer: Continentality increases seasonal temperature extremes.

questions.question: The narrow water body separating Asia from North America is the _____.
questions.answer: Bering Strait separates Siberia from Alaska.

questions.question: The world's largest inland lake is the _____.
questions.answer: Caspian Sea is largest by area.

questions.question: The highest and largest plateau of the world is the _____.
questions.answer: Tibet is the highest and largest plateau.

questions.question: The warm current off Japan is the _____.
questions.answer: Kuroshio is the warm current; Oyashio is cold.

questions.question: The south-west monsoon brings _____ rainfall to South Asia.
questions.answer: SW monsoon is the summer rainy season.

questions.question: Match mountain range with location:
questions.answer: Himalayas-South Asia; Zagros-Iran; Arakan-Myanmar; Altai-Central Asia/Siberia

questions.question: Match river with ocean basin:
questions.answer: Lena-Arctic; Yangtze-Pacific; Ganga-Indian; Tigris-Persian Gulf

questions.question: Match lake with feature:
questions.answer: Caspian-largest; Baikal-deepest; Aral-shrinking; Dead Sea-below sea level

questions.question: Match physical division with example:
questions.answer: North-West Siberian; Central-Himalayas; South plateau-Deccan; South plains-Indo-Gangetic

questions.question: Match climate factor with effect:
questions.answer: Latitude-temp; Altitude-cooler; Continentality-extremes; Relief-rain shadow

questions.question: Match climate zone with region:
questions.answer: Tundra-north Siberia; Taiga-central Siberia; Med-Turkey; Equatorial-Indonesia

questions.question: Match vegetation type with climate:
questions.answer: Coniferous-subarctic; Steppe-semi-arid; Mangroves-delta; Tundra-polar

questions.question: Match plain with river system:
questions.answer: North China-Huang He/Yangtze; Indo-Gangetic-Indus/Ganga/Brahmaputra; Mesopotamian-Tigris/Euphrates; West Siberian-Ob/Yenisei

questions.question: Match monsoon term with description:
questions.answer: SW monsoon-summer; NE monsoon-winter; Western disturbances-winter rain; Typhoon-Pacific cyclone

questions.question: Match desert with location:
questions.answer: Arabian-West Asia; Gobi-Mongolia/China; Taklamakan-Xinjiang; Thar-India/Pakistan

questions.question: State the boundaries of Asia and name the feature separating it from North America.
questions.answer: Asia is bounded by the Arctic Ocean to the north, the Indian Ocean to the south, the Pacific Ocean to the east and the Mediterranean–Ural chain to the west (Ural Mountains, Ural River, Caspian Sea, Caucasus and Black Sea). The Bering Strait separates Asia from North America.

questions.question: Describe Asia's three major physical divisions with one example each.
questions.answer: Asia has Northern Lowlands (e.g., West Siberian Plain), Central Highlands (e.g., Himalayas or Tibetan Plateau) and Southern Plateaus/Plains (e.g., Deccan Plateau and Indo-Gangetic Plain). These divisions influence climate, rivers and settlement.

questions.question: Explain the role of the Himalayas in South Asia's climate.
questions.answer: The Himalayas block cold winds from Central Asia, keeping northern India warmer in winter. They also force moist south-west monsoon winds to rise, producing heavy orographic rainfall on the windward slopes and sustaining major rivers.

questions.question: Differentiate between south-west and north-east monsoons.
questions.answer: The south-west monsoon blows from sea to land in summer, bringing heavy rainfall to South and Southeast Asia. The north-east monsoon blows from land to sea in winter and is generally dry, except along the Tamil Nadu coast where it picks up moisture from the Bay of Bengal.

questions.question: Name two important rivers and two important lakes of Asia with one feature each.
questions.answer: Rivers: Yangtze (Asia's longest, drains into Pacific) and Ganga (forms fertile Indo-Gangetic Plain). Lakes: Caspian Sea (largest inland lake) and Lake Baikal (deepest freshwater lake).
